questions.title: The Offside Disagreement
questions.scenario: The referee calls offside on a play you are certain was onside. Your team had a clear chance to score. You...

options.text: Take a deep breath and wait for the next play.
Stand up and shout “He was onside!” so everyone nearby hears you.
Cup your hands and yell “Get some glasses, referee!”
Spend the next ten minutes loudly mocking every decision the referee makes.

questions.reveal: RAP Level 1 — Verbal Taunting[cite: 18, 87]. Questioning a referee's vision [cite: 95] or competence [cite: 93] is a direct example of Level 1 abuse. Under U.S. Soccer policy, this carries a minimum 2-game suspension[cite: 40, 108].

questions.title: The Post-Match Departure
questions.scenario: Your team loses the game on a late penalty call. The match ended 10 minutes ago. You...

options.text: Tell the players “well done” and head home. It’s just a game.
Stare at the referee while packing your bags to make sure they feel your disapproval.
Wait near the exit to have a “serious word” with the referee about their performance.
Follow the referee toward their car or the changing rooms to continue arguing.

questions.reveal: RAP Physical Level 2 — Confrontation[cite: 217]. Following a referee to their vehicle [cite: 230] or private space is Physical Level 2 abuse. This carries a minimum 10-game suspension[cite: 42, 236].

questions.title: The Online Critic
questions.scenario: A video of a controversial call from today's match is posted on a social media group. You...

options.text: Keep scrolling. You have better things to do.
Post a simple comment saying “Bad call” and move on.
Write a long post tagging the league and demanding the referee be banned.
Find the referee’s personal profile and send them a private message criticizing them.

questions.reveal: RAP Level 3 — Cyberbullying[cite: 22, 138]. Directing personal attacks or harassment at a referee online [cite: 148] is Level 3 abuse. This can lead to a 6–24 month suspension[cite: 40, 154].

questions.title: The Bias Accusation
questions.scenario: It feels like every 50/50 challenge is being called against your team. You...

options.text: Accept that the referee has a different angle than you do.
Turn to the person next to you and complain about the consistency.
Shout “Call it both ways!” at the top of your lungs.
Yell “How much is the other team paying you?!”

questions.reveal: RAP Level 1 — Verbal Taunting[cite: 18, 87]. Accusing a referee of being paid off [cite: 97] or biased [cite: 96] is a direct example of undermining their authority[cite: 90]. It is a Level 1 offense[cite: 18].

questions.title: The Emotional Outburst
questions.scenario: A very obvious foul is missed right in front of you. Your immediate reaction is to...

options.text: Clap sarcastically once and then sit back down.
Stand up and throw your arms out in frustration.
Pace the sideline yelling angry, disrespectful comments.
Scream insults and profanities directly at the referee.

questions.reveal: RAP Level 2 — Harassment[cite: 20, 115]. Using aggressive language [cite: 118] or profanity [cite: 120] to intimidate a referee is Level 2 abuse. It carries a minimum 4-game suspension[cite: 40, 132].

questions.title: The Junior Referee
questions.scenario: A referee who looks like a teenager makes a mistake. You...

options.text: Stay quiet. They are a young person still learning the job.
Sigh loudly so they hear you, but don't say anything specific.
Yell “Learn the rules!” like you would to an adult.
Mock their age, calling them “too young” or “clueless.”

questions.reveal: Minor Multiplier[cite: 49]. Under the RAP policy, offenses against minor referees [cite: 49] are automatically subject to triple punishment[cite: 49].

questions.title: The Personal Space Test
questions.scenario: You want to ask the referee about a call during a break. You...

options.text: Wait for a calm moment and ask respectfully from a distance.
Call out to them from the sideline while they are running past.
Stand in their path as they walk to the sideline so they have to stop.
Get inside their personal space (face-to-face) to make your point.

questions.reveal: RAP Level 2 — Intimidation[cite: 20, 115]. Using your body language or invading a referee's face [cite: 126] to make them feel unsafe is Level 2 abuse[cite: 116].

questions.title: The Parking Lot Threat
questions.scenario: In a moment of extreme anger after a red card, what is the most aggressive thing you might say?

options.text: “I don't agree with that call.”
“You’re having a terrible match today.”
“You’re going to regret making that call!”
“I’ll see you in the parking lot later.”

questions.reveal: RAP Level 3 — Threats[cite: 22, 138]. Threatening to meet a referee in the parking lot [cite: 145] or telling them they will 'pay' for a call [cite: 144] is Level 3 abuse[cite: 141].

questions.title: The Attention Grabber
questions.scenario: The referee is walking away. You want their attention, so you...

options.text: Let it go and talk to a league representative later.
Shout their name or “Ref!” loudly.
Reach out and tap them on the shoulder or arm.
Grab their jersey to stop them from walking away.

questions.reveal: RAP Physical Level 1 — Minor Contact[cite: 27, 195]. Any deliberate, unwanted touching [cite: 196], such as tapping a shoulder [cite: 200] or grabbing a jersey[cite: 202], is physical abuse.

questions.title: The Pitch Invasion
questions.scenario: An opponent commits a dangerous foul on your team, and the referee gives no card. You...

options.text: Stay on the sideline and wait for the match to continue.
Yell at the referee from the sideline about the lack of a card.
Step onto the field of play to show the referee your anger.
Run onto the pitch to confront the referee directly.

questions.reveal: RAP Physical Level 2 — Confrontation[cite: 29, 217]. Entering the field to confront an official is an intentional confrontation [cite: 217] and is a Level 2 physical offense[cite: 29].

questions.title: The Boundary Line Dispute
questions.scenario: The ball goes out for a throw-in. You are sure it is your ball, but the referee gives it to the opponent. You...

options.text: Accept the decision and focus on the defense.
Mutter “it was our ball” to yourself.
Shout “It’s our ball! Learn the lines, ref!”
Spend the rest of the half shouting about the referee's incompetence.

questions.reveal: RAP Level 1 — Verbal Taunting[cite: 18, 87]. This behavior undermines the referee's authority [cite: 90] and ability to officiate the game[cite: 88].

questions.title: The Object Throw
questions.scenario: The game ends in a way you find completely unfair. As the referee walks off, you...

options.text: Pack your things and leave.
Call out “Terrible job today!”
Throw a water bottle toward the referee in frustration.
Try to physically push or strike the referee.

questions.reveal: RAP Physical Level 3 — Violent Contact[cite: 31, 245]. Throwing objects with intent to hit [cite: 256] or striking [cite: 251] an official can result in a lifetime ban[cite: 42, 263].

questions.title: The Dignity Rule
questions.scenario: In the heat of an argument, you use a slur or comment about the referee’s nationality or identity. You...

options.text: I would never do this; it's just a sport.
I might make a comment but immediately apologize.
I think offensive comments are part of stadium culture.
I use whatever language is most hurtful to win the argument.

questions.reveal: RAP Level 4 — Discriminatory Act[cite: 24, 163]. Attacks on race, skin color, nationality, or identity [cite: 167] have no place in soccer and carry a minimum 10-game/12-month suspension[cite: 40, 177, 178].

questions.title: The Equipment Tantrum
questions.scenario: You are angry at a call and see the referee's bag or the corner flag nearby. You...

options.text: Leave it alone.
Kick the grass near it.
Knock over the equipment in frustration.
Damage or throw the referee’s personal gear.

questions.reveal: RAP Physical Level 2 — Property Damage[cite: 42, 223]. Damaging property [cite: 223], such as throwing equipment [cite: 227] or tampering with a bag [cite: 228], is Level 2 abuse[cite: 217].

questions.title: The Sarcastic Clap
questions.scenario: The referee gives a card to your player. You stand up and clap sarcastically while staring at them. You...

options.text: I stay seated and quiet.
I shake my head but don't clap.
I give one loud, sarcastic clap.
I applaud and shout “Brilliant job!” for two minutes straight.

questions.reveal: RAP Level 2 — Intimidation[cite: 20, 115]. Gestures intended to belittle the referee [cite: 88] fall under the harassment framework[cite: 118].

questions.title: The Rule Expert
questions.scenario: A foul is called. You shout “Do you even know the rules?!” at the referee. You...

options.text: I never question their knowledge mid-game.
I might say it quietly to another parent.
I say it once when I'm frustrated.
I repeat it every time there is a whistle.

questions.reveal: RAP Level 1 — Verbal Taunting[cite: 18, 87]. Questioning the referee's competence [cite: 92, 93] is cited as Level 1 abuse[cite: 87].

questions.title: The Shoulder Brush
questions.scenario: While walking past the referee, you intentionally brush your shoulder against theirs. You...

options.text: I give them plenty of space.
I walk close but don't touch.
It was an 'accident,' I might have brushed them.
I intentionally bump them to show I'm there.

questions.reveal: RAP Physical Level 1 — Minor Contact[cite: 27, 195]. Intentionally bumping [cite: 203] or brushing against an official [cite: 196] is physical abuse.

questions.title: The Sideline Pacing
questions.scenario: A call goes against you. You begin pacing the touchline, yelling and gesturing wildly at the referee. You...

options.text: I stay in my seat or area.
I stand up but stay calm.
I walk a few steps while complaining.
I follow the referee up and down the line while yelling.

questions.reveal: RAP Level 2 — Harassment[cite: 20, 115]. This behavior is intended to make a referee feel unsafe [cite: 116] and is classified as intimidation[cite: 115].

questions.title: The Digital Threat
questions.scenario: You post on a public forum that the referee “better watch their back” after that performance. You...

options.text: I would never post that.
I might vent without the threat.
I post it but delete it later.
I post it and hope they see it.

questions.reveal: RAP Level 3 — Threats[cite: 22, 138]. Using language like “watch your back” [cite: 147] online [cite: 141] is a serious Level 3 offense[cite: 138].

questions.title: The Water Splash
questions.scenario: You are holding a water bottle and, in anger, you spray water toward the referee. You...

options.text: I keep my hands to myself.
I squeeze the bottle but not at the ref.
I spray water on the ground near them.
I spray water directly at them.

questions.reveal: RAP Physical Level 2 — Intentional Confrontation[cite: 29, 217]. Using an object in a non-striking manner [cite: 222], like spraying water [cite: 227], is Level 2 physical abuse[cite: 217].

questions.title: The Insinuation
questions.scenario: You yell “Who’s on your team, ref?!” after a foul is called. You...

options.text: I never imply bias.
I might think it, but don't say it.
I shout it once when I'm mad.
I say it repeatedly to annoy them.

questions.reveal: RAP Level 1 — Verbal Taunting[cite: 18, 87]. Insinuating behavior [cite: 90] that undermines referee authority [cite: 88] is a Level 1 offense[cite: 18].

questions.title: The Face-to-Face
questions.scenario: During halftime, you get into the referee’s face to tell them they are doing a “pathetic job.” You...

options.text: I don't approach them.
I talk from the sideline.
I approach them but stay 2 meters away.
I get as close as possible without touching.

questions.reveal: RAP Level 2 — Intimidation[cite: 20, 115]. Getting in the referee's face [cite: 126] is specifically listed as Level 2 harassment/intimidation[cite: 115].

questions.title: The Parking Lot Standoff
questions.scenario: You wait by the gate as the referee is leaving and shout “You won't get away with this!” You...

options.text: I leave as soon as the whistle blows.
I might wait but don't say anything.
I yell from my car.
I stand in their way and shout threats.

questions.reveal: RAP Level 3 — Threats[cite: 22, 138]. Aggressive language [cite: 141] and threats [cite: 144] in the parking lot area are Level 3 offenses[cite: 138].

questions.title: The Finger Point
questions.scenario: While arguing, you poke your finger toward the referee and touch their chest. You...

options.text: I keep my distance.
I point but don't touch.
I might have accidentally touched them.
I intentionally poke them to make a point.

questions.reveal: RAP Physical Level 1 — Minor Contact[cite: 27, 195]. Touching a referee's chest [cite: 201] is a specific example of Level 1 physical abuse[cite: 195].

questions.title: The Mocking Tone
questions.scenario: Every time the referee speaks, you repeat what they say in a high-pitched, mocking voice. You...

options.text: I don't do this.
I do it quietly once.
I do it loudly a few times.
I do it the whole game to embarrass them.

questions.reveal: RAP Level 1 — Verbal Taunting[cite: 18, 87]. Mocking [cite: 94] or belittling speech [cite: 88] is abuse[cite: 87]. If it becomes aggressive, it moves to Level 2[cite: 115].

questions.title: The Stereotype
questions.scenario: You make a comment about a referee’s gender, implying they don’t understand the game. You...

options.text: I never judge by identity.
I might think it but stay silent.
I say it to a friend nearby.
I shout it at the referee.

questions.reveal: RAP Level 4 — Discriminatory Act[cite: 24, 163]. Using harmful stereotypes [cite: 172] based on gender [cite: 167] is a Level 4 offense[cite: 163].

questions.title: The Arm Grab
questions.scenario: The referee is trying to move away, and you grab their arm to keep talking to them. You...

options.text: I don't touch them.
I reach but don't grab.
I grab them lightly.
I grab them forcefully.

questions.reveal: RAP Physical Level 2 — Intentional Confrontation[cite: 29, 217]. Grabbing an official [cite: 221], especially forcefully [cite: 226], is Level 2 physical abuse[cite: 217].

questions.title: The Dismissive Comment
questions.scenario: The referee makes a call, and you shout “You’re a joke!” You...

options.text: I stay respectful.
I mutter it.
I shout it once.
I yell it after every call.

questions.reveal: RAP Level 1 — Verbal Taunting[cite: 18, 87]. Using dismissive language [cite: 98] like “you suck” [cite: 99] or “you're a joke” [cite: 123] is abuse[cite: 87, 115].

questions.title: The Identity Hunt
questions.scenario: You search online to find the referee's home town or employer to include in your complaint. You...

options.text: I don't care about their personal life.
I only look for their name.
I look for their details out of curiosity.
I do it to show them I know who they are.

questions.reveal: RAP Level 3 — Doxing[cite: 22, 138]. Doxing [cite: 141] or tracking personal details [cite: 141] for the purpose of harassment is a Level 3 offense[cite: 138].

questions.title: The Ball Kick
questions.scenario: In a fit of rage, you kick a spare ball onto the field toward the referee. You...

options.text: I don't touch the balls.
I kick it to the side.
I kick it near them on purpose.
I kick it at them with force.

questions.reveal: RAP Physical Level 2 — Intentional Confrontation[cite: 29, 217]. Kicking a ball with intent to hit [cite: 257] or disrupt [cite: 222] is Level 2 or 3 abuse[cite: 217, 245].

questions.title: The Threatening Stare
questions.scenario: You stand alone and stare intensely at the referee for several minutes without saying a word. You...

options.text: I watch the game.
I look at them briefly.
I stare them down when they are near.
I make eye contact and don't look away.

questions.reveal: RAP Level 2 — Intimidation[cite: 20, 115]. Body language intended to make a referee feel unsafe [cite: 116] is classified as Level 2 abuse[cite: 115].

questions.title: The Religion Jab
questions.scenario: You make a derogatory comment about the referee’s religious symbols or clothing. You...

options.text: I would never do that.
I might think it.
I make a joke about it.
I use it as an insult.

questions.reveal: RAP Level 4 — Discriminatory Act[cite: 24, 163]. Discrimination based on religion [cite: 167] is a Level 4 offense [cite: 163] and carries heavy penalties[cite: 177, 178].

questions.title: The Attention Tap
questions.scenario: You tap the referee’s back to tell them they missed a foul. You...

options.text: I don't touch them.
I call out from far away.
I tap them once.
I poke them repeatedly.

questions.reveal: RAP Physical Level 1 — Minor Contact[cite: 27, 195]. Tapping a shoulder [cite: 200] or back is considered slight deliberate touching [cite: 198] and is abuse[cite: 195].

questions.title: The Appearance Mock
questions.scenario: You yell something about the referee’s physical fitness or speed. You...

options.text: I don't judge their appearance.
I think it but stay silent.
I make a joke to a friend.
I yell it at the referee.

questions.reveal: RAP Level 1 — Verbal Taunting[cite: 18, 87]. Mocking abilities [cite: 94] or appearance [cite: 94] is listed as Level 1 abuse[cite: 87].

questions.title: The Flag Tug
questions.scenario: You are standing near the assistant referee and tug on their flag to get their attention. You...

options.text: I stay away from their equipment.
I call out to them.
I touch the flag.
I pull the flag forcefully.

questions.reveal: RAP Physical Level 2 — Intentional Confrontation[cite: 29, 217]. Pulling [cite: 221] or grabbing equipment [cite: 222] is a Level 2 physical offense[cite: 217].

questions.title: The Throat Gesture
questions.scenario: You make a throat-cutting gesture at the referee after a bad call. You...

options.text: I don't make aggressive gestures.
I make a thumbs-down gesture.
I do it quickly once.
I do it and hold the pose.

questions.reveal: RAP Level 3 — Threats[cite: 22, 138]. Gestures that threaten physical violence [cite: 141] are Level 3 offenses[cite: 138].

questions.title: The Path Block
questions.scenario: The referee is trying to leave the field. You stand directly in the gate so they cannot pass. You...

options.text: I give them plenty of space.
I stand near the path.
I make them walk around me.
I physically block them with my body.

questions.reveal: RAP Level 2 — Intimidation[cite: 20, 115]. Using body language to block a path [cite: 116] is intimidation[cite: 115].

questions.title: The Jersey Pull
questions.scenario: You pull on the referee’s jersey during a heated discussion. You...

options.text: I don't touch them.
I reach for them but stop.
I pull it once.
I pull it and won't let go.

questions.reveal: RAP Physical Level 1 — Minor Contact[cite: 27, 195]. Grabbing a jersey [cite: 202] is a specific example of Level 1 physical abuse[cite: 195].

questions.title: The Tone Test
questions.scenario: You speak to the referee in a loud, aggressive tone of voice. You...

options.text: I keep my voice calm.
I speak firmly.
I raise my voice.
I yell as loud as I can.

questions.reveal: RAP Level 1 — Verbal Taunting[cite: 18, 87]. Using an aggressive tone [cite: 100] is listed as abuse[cite: 87].

questions.title: The Ultimate Line
questions.scenario: You become so angry that you consider spitting toward or at the referee. You...

options.text: I would never consider this.
I spit on the ground far away.
I spit toward them but not to hit them.
I spit at them directly.

questions.reveal: RAP Physical Level 3 — Violent Contact[cite: 31, 245]. Spitting [cite: 249] is a violent Level 3 offense [cite: 245] and carries the severest penalties[cite: 263].

tiers.title: The Referee's Dream
tiers.desc: You are a rare find. You respect the game and the people who run it. Keep it up.

tiers.title: Mostly Harmless
tiers.desc: You have your moments of frustration, but you stay within the bounds of respect.

tiers.title: Yellow Card Territory
tiers.desc: You are starting to cross the line. Your behavior is becoming a problem for the officials.

tiers.title: Red Card Energy
tiers.desc: You are in official abuse territory. You are likely to face suspensions under the RAP policy.

tiers.title: Serious Consequences
tiers.desc: Your behavior is harmful to the sport. You face multi-month bans.

tiers.title: Lifetime Ban Loading...
tiers.desc: You are part of the reason officials walk away. You face permanent exclusion from the sport.
